intro: Canneh was an ancient trading city mentioned in Ezekiel's lament over Tyre (Ezekiel 27:23) as one of several Mesopotamian or Arabian trading partners that supplied goods to the Phoenician port. It is listed alongside Haran, Eden, Asshur, and Chilmad as a source of merchandise. The precise identification of Canneh is uncertain; some scholars equate it with Calneh of Genesis 10:10, while others suggest it was a distinct city in the northern Euphrates region or in Arabia. Its mention in the context of Tyre's extensive trade network attests to the wide commercial reach of the ancient Phoenician economy.
